name: Nmap
description: Industry-standard network scanner for host discovery, port scanning, and service/version detection across a target network.

name: theHarvester
description: OSINT reconnaissance tool that gathers emails, subdomains, hosts, and employee names from public sources and search engines.

name: Amass
description: In-depth attack surface mapping tool for subdomain enumeration, DNS resolution, and network asset discovery.

name: Masscan
description: Extremely high-speed port scanner capable of scanning the entire internet address space in minutes.

name: RustScan
description: Fast modern port scanner written in Rust, designed to pipe results directly into Nmap for deeper analysis.

name: Holehe
description: Checks if an email address is registered on popular websites by exploiting password-reset and registration endpoints.

name: Maigret
description: Collects a person's digital footprint by searching for their username across hundreds of online platforms.

name: httpx
description: Fast and multi-purpose HTTP toolkit that probes live hosts, technologies, and response metadata at scale.

name: SpiderFoot
description: Automated OSINT reconnaissance platform that aggregates data from over 200 sources into a single footprint report.

name: Subfinder
description: Passive subdomain discovery tool that queries multiple public sources to build a target's subdomain map.

name: TruffleHog
description: Scans git repositories and file systems for accidentally committed secrets, API keys, and credentials.

name: Gitleaks
description: Lightweight secret-scanning tool that audits git history for hardcoded passwords, tokens, and private keys.

name: Dracnmap
description: Automation wrapper around Nmap that streamlines common reconnaissance scan sequences.

name: Xerosploit
description: Penetration testing toolkit for network attacks including scanning, sniffing, and traffic analysis.

name: RED HAWK
description: All-in-one reconnaissance tool combining information gathering, vulnerability scanning, and crawling.

examples.title: Run basic scan with RED HAWK

name: ReconSpider
description: OSINT framework that consolidates reconnaissance across IPs, emails, domains, and phone numbers.

name: Infoga
description: Email OSINT tool that gathers account information linked to a target email address from public sources.

name: ReconDog
description: Reconnaissance suite that acts as a passive information-gathering tool for footprinting targets.

name: Striker
description: Offensive reconnaissance tool that automatically performs a chain of scans against a target domain.

name: SecretFinder
description: Searches JavaScript files for exposed API keys, tokens, and other sensitive endpoints.

name: Shodanfy
description: Command-line client that queries the Shodan search engine for exposed devices and services tied to a target.

name: rang3r
description: IP range and CIDR-based information gathering tool for network reconnaissance.

name: Breacher
description: Locates hidden admin login panels on target web applications.

name: Hashcat
description: World's fastest password recovery tool, using GPU acceleration to crack hashes from captured credential data.

name: John the Ripper
description: Classic offline password cracker supporting dozens of hash types with dictionary and brute-force modes.

examples.title: Run basic scan with John the Ripper

name: haiti
description: Hash type identifier that analyzes a hash string and suggests which algorithm produced it.

name: Cupp
description: Generates targeted wordlists based on personal information about a specific individual for social-engineering audits.

name: Goblin WordGenerator
description: Custom wordlist generator for building password dictionaries from patterns and rules.

examples.title: Run basic scan with Goblin WordGenerator

name: Airgeddon
description: Multi-use Bash script for wireless network auditing, from handshake capture to WPA/WPA2 security testing.

name: Bettercap
description: Swiss-army-knife framework for WiFi, Bluetooth, and network reconnaissance and man-in-the-middle testing.

name: hcxdumptool
description: Captures WiFi handshakes and PMKID data for offline WPA/WPA2 security analysis.

name: hcxtools
description: Companion toolset for converting captured WiFi traffic into crackable hash formats.

name: WiFi-Pumpkin
description: Rogue access point framework for simulating WiFi security assessments.

name: Wifite
description: Automated wireless auditing tool that attacks multiple WEP/WPA/WPS networks in sequence.

name: Fluxion
description: Evil-twin attack framework used to audit WiFi network credential security.

name: pixiewps
description: Offline WPS PIN recovery tool exploiting weak randomness in some router implementations.

name: Wifiphisher
description: Rogue access point tool for conducting WiFi security awareness and credential-capture assessments.

name: Sqlmap
description: The definitive open-source tool for detecting and testing SQL injection vulnerabilities in web applications.

name: NoSqlMap
description: Automated NoSQL injection and database enumeration testing tool for MongoDB and similar databases.

name: DSSS
description: Minimal SQL injection scanner in under 100 lines, useful for quick vulnerability triage.

name: Leviathan
description: Wide-ranging audit toolkit combining mass scanning with SQL injection and service fingerprinting.

name: Nuclei
description: Template-based vulnerability scanner that checks web applications against thousands of community-curated CVE and misconfiguration templates.

name: ffuf
description: Fast web fuzzer for directory, parameter, and virtual host discovery on target applications.

name: Feroxbuster
description: Recursive content-discovery tool that brute-forces directories and files across a web application.

name: Nikto
description: Web server scanner that checks for dangerous files, outdated software, and common misconfigurations.

name: Gobuster
description: Directory, DNS, and virtual host brute-forcing tool built for speed.

name: OWASP ZAP
description: Full-featured web application security scanner maintained by OWASP, covering both automated and manual testing.

examples.title: Run basic scan with OWASP ZAP

name: Dirsearch
description: Command-line web path scanner for brute-forcing hidden directories and files.

name: Katana
description: Fast crawling framework for discovering endpoints and application attack surface.

name: wafw00f
description: Fingerprints and identifies which Web Application Firewall (if any) is protecting a target site.

name: testssl.sh
description: Checks a server's TLS/SSL configuration for known vulnerabilities, cipher weaknesses, and certificate issues.

name: Arjun
description: HTTP parameter discovery tool that finds hidden query and form parameters accepted by an endpoint.

name: mitmproxy
description: Interactive proxy for inspecting, modifying, and replaying HTTP/HTTPS traffic during security testing.

name: Sublist3r
description: Subdomain enumeration tool that aggregates results from multiple search engines.

name: Caido
description: Modern web security auditing toolkit offering proxy interception and automated scanning workflows.

name: Dirb
description: Classic content scanner that brute-forces web directories using wordlists.

name: Sub-Domain TakeOver
description: Detects subdomains vulnerable to takeover due to dangling DNS records pointing to unclaimed services.

examples.title: Run basic scan with Sub-Domain TakeOver

name: CheckURL
description: Utility for checking URL status, redirects, and reachability during reconnaissance.

name: Setoolkit
description: Widely-referenced social engineering framework used in security-awareness training to demonstrate phishing risk.

name: dnstwist
description: Detects typosquatting and lookalike domains that could be used to impersonate a brand.

name: QRLJacking
description: Documents QR-code login hijacking attack patterns for awareness and defensive research.

name: Maskphish
description: Demonstrates URL-masking techniques used in phishing awareness training.

name: PEASS-ng (LinPEAS/WinPEAS)
description: Privilege-escalation enumeration scripts that scan a compromised host for common misconfigurations leading to elevated access.

name: Sliver
description: Open-source command-and-control framework used in adversary-simulation and red-team engagements.

name: Havoc
description: Modern, modular command-and-control framework for red-team post-exploitation operations.

name: pwncat-cs
description: Post-exploitation platform that upgrades basic shells into fully-featured interactive sessions.

name: Ligolo-ng
description: Tunneling tool for pivoting through compromised networks during authorized engagements.

name: Chisel
description: Fast TCP/UDP tunnel over HTTP, commonly used for network pivoting during security assessments.

name: Evil-WinRM
description: Windows Remote Management shell client used for authorized remote administration testing.

name: Mythic
description: Collaborative, cross-platform red-team command-and-control framework with a plugin architecture.

name: Volatility 3
description: Advanced memory forensics framework for extracting artifacts from RAM captures during incident response.

name: Binwalk
description: Firmware analysis tool for identifying and extracting embedded files and executable code from binary images.

name: Wireshark
description: The standard network protocol analyzer for capturing and inspecting traffic at the packet level.

name: Bulk extractor
description: Scans disk images and files to extract forensic artifacts like emails, URLs, and credit card numbers.

examples.title: Run basic scan with Bulk extractor

name: pspy
description: Monitors Linux process activity without requiring root, useful for spotting suspicious scheduled tasks.

name: RouterSploit
description: Exploitation framework focused specifically on embedded devices and routers.

name: Commix
description: Automated tool for detecting and exploiting command-injection vulnerabilities in web applications.

name: WebSploit
description: Modular framework combining several web and network exploitation modules under one interface.

name: Ghidra
description: NSA-developed software reverse-engineering suite for disassembling and decompiling binaries.

name: Radare2
description: Command-line reverse-engineering framework for binary analysis, disassembly, and debugging.

name: Androguard
description: Python framework for reverse-engineering and analyzing Android application packages.

name: JadX
description: Decompiler that converts Android DEX bytecode back into readable Java source.

name: Apk2Gold
description: Utility for converting Android APK files into Java source for analysis.

name: BloodHound
description: Graph-based analysis tool that reveals hidden attack paths and privilege-escalation chains inside Active Directory environments.

name: Impacket
description: Python library implementing network protocols used across Windows and Active Directory security testing.

name: NetExec (nxc)
description: Network-service exploitation and enumeration swiss-army-knife for large Windows environments.

name: Responder
description: LLMNR/NBT-NS/MDNS poisoner used to capture credentials during internal network assessments.

name: Certipy
description: Toolkit for enumerating and abusing misconfigurations in Active Directory Certificate Services.

name: Kerbrute
description: Fast tool for enumerating and brute-forcing Kerberos accounts in Active Directory.

name: Prowler
description: Cloud security assessment tool that audits AWS, Azure, and GCP environments against compliance benchmarks.

name: ScoutSuite
description: Multi-cloud security auditing tool that generates a visual report of misconfigurations across providers.

name: Pacu
description: AWS exploitation framework used to test the security posture of cloud environments.

name: Trivy
description: Comprehensive vulnerability scanner for container images, filesystems, and infrastructure-as-code.

name: MobSF
description: Automated mobile application security testing framework for Android, iOS, and Windows apps.

name: Frida
description: Dynamic instrumentation toolkit for injecting scripts into running applications during mobile security testing.

name: Objection
description: Runtime mobile exploration toolkit built on Frida for assessing mobile app security without jailbreak.

name: SlowLoris
description: Documents a well-known low-bandwidth denial-of-service technique used in defensive capacity-planning research.

name: StegoCracker
description: Brute-force tool for cracking passwords on steganographically hidden data within images.

name: Whitespace
description: Hides and extracts data encoded within whitespace characters of text files.

name: DalFox
description: Fast parameter analysis and XSS scanning tool built for automated pipelines.

name: XSStrike
description: Advanced XSS detection suite with a fuzzing engine and context-aware payload generation for research purposes.

name: Sherlock
description: Hunts down usernames across hundreds of social networks to map an individual's online presence.

name: SocialScan
description: Checks email address and username availability across social platforms without triggering notifications.
